study_title: Effect of the fungicide pyraclostrobin on the transcriptome of Africanized honey bees Apis mellifera during their foraging phase
exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: adult brain tissue
design: The total RNA was extracted from the brain pools of nurse bees A. mellifera using the TRIzol reagent protocol according to the manufacturers instructions (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). RNA concentrations were quantified in each sample using a QubitTM 2.0 fluorometer (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). The levels of degradation of RNA were assessed by a 1% agarose gel. The cDNA libraries were constructed with 200 ng of total RNA using the SureSelect Strand Specific RNA Library Preparation Kit (Agilent Technologies, Santa Clara, USA), following the manufacturers instructions. The products from the library were then sequenced using an Illumina Nextseq platform (Illumina, San Diego, USA) in a single-bore run of 150 bp.

exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: adult brain tissue
design: The total RNA was extracted from the brain pools of nurse bees A. mellifera using the TRIzol reagent protocol according to the manufacturers instructions (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). RNA concentrations were quantified in each sample using a QubitTM 2.0 fluorometer (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). The levels of degradation of RNA were assessed by a 1% agarose gel. The cDNA libraries were constructed with 200 ng of total RNA using the SureSelect Strand Specific RNA Library Preparation Kit (Agilent Technologies, Santa Clara, USA), following the manufacturers instructions. The products from the library were then sequenced using an Illumina Nextseq platform (Illumina, San Diego, USA) in a single-bore run of 150 bp.

RNA concentrations were quantified in each sample using a QubitTM 2.0 fluorometer (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). The levels of degradation of RNA were assessed by a 1% agarose gel. The cDNA libraries were constructed with 200 ng of total RNA using the SureSelect Strand Specific RNA Library Preparation Kit (Agilent Technologies, Santa Clara, USA), following the manufacturers instructions. The products from the library were then sequenced using an Illumina Nextseq platform (Illumina, San Diego, USA) in a single-bore run of 150 bp.

exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: adult brain tissue
design: The total RNA was extracted from the brain pools of nurse bees A. mellifera using the TRIzol reagent protocol according to the manufacturers instructions (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). RNA concentrations were quantified in each sample using a QubitTM 2.0 fluorometer (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). The levels of degradation of RNA were assessed by a 1% agarose gel. The cDNA libraries were constructed with 200 ng of total RNA using the SureSelect Strand Specific RNA Library Preparation Kit (Agilent Technologies, Santa Clara, USA), following the manufacturers instructions. The products from the library were then sequenced using an Illumina Nextseq platform (Illumina, San Diego, USA) in a single-bore run of 150 bp.

exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: thorax of young adult treated with Imidacloprid at a target dose of 100 pg per bee for 48H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: adult brain tissue
design: The total RNA was extracted from the brain pools of nurse bees A. mellifera using the TRIzol reagent protocol according to the manufacturers instructions (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). RNA concentrations were quantified in each sample using a QubitTM 2.0 fluorometer (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). The levels of degradation of RNA were assessed by a 1% agarose gel. The cDNA libraries were constructed with 200 ng of total RNA using the SureSelect Strand Specific RNA Library Preparation Kit (Agilent Technologies, Santa Clara, USA), following the manufacturers instructions. The products from the library were then sequenced using an Illumina Nextseq platform (Illumina, San Diego, USA) in a single-bore run of 150 bp.

exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: fat-body of young adult treated with Imidacloprid at a target dose of 1000 pg per bee for 48H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

study_title: New Approach Methodologies for Understanding Honey Bee Toxicity
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: abdomen of young adult treated with Fipronil at a target dose of 3000 pg per bee for 48H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: adult brain tissue
design: The total RNA was extracted from the brain pools of nurse bees A. mellifera using the TRIzol reagent protocol according to the manufacturers instructions (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). RNA concentrations were quantified in each sample using a QubitTM 2.0 fluorometer (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). The levels of degradation of RNA were assessed by a 1% agarose gel. The cDNA libraries were constructed with 200 ng of total RNA using the SureSelect Strand Specific RNA Library Preparation Kit (Agilent Technologies, Santa Clara, USA), following the manufacturers instructions. The products from the library were then sequenced using an Illumina Nextseq platform (Illumina, San Diego, USA) in a single-bore run of 150 bp.

study_title: Transcriptomic analysis of the honey bee (Apis mellifera) queen brain in response to pesticide exposure during development
design: 1. CONTAMINATED QUEEN CUPS WITH PESTICIDES: Plastic queen cups were coated with 200 mg of molten bees wax that was clean or mixed with pesticides in treatment groups. The pesticides used were: C (Control-No Pesticide), A (Amitraz-~46,820 PPB), CC (Chlorothalonil + Chlorphyrifos - 53,700:890 PPB), FC (Fluvalinate + Coumophos - 20,400:93,200 PPB). 2. GRAFTED LARVAE TO RAISE QUEENS: One-day-old worker larvae were carefully transferred to the wax-coated queen cups. Developing queen cells were then placed into queenless cell builders with nurse workers that reared the future queens. 3. TRANSFERED NEW QUEENS TO NEW COLONIES: Emerged queens were individually transferred to mating nucleus colonies to allow them to mate naturally and lay fertilized eggs. 4. CAPTUREED QUEENS AND ISOLATE BRAIN: Mated queens were sacrificed and brains were dissected into Direct-zol(TM). 5. RNA EXTRACTION: RNA isolated using Direct-zol(TM) and glycogen as a coprecipitator. Quantified via HS RNA Qubit 2.0.

study_title: Transcriptomic analysis of the honey bee (Apis mellifera) queen brain in response to pesticide exposure during development
design: 1. CONTAMINATED QUEEN CUPS WITH PESTICIDES: Plastic queen cups were coated with 200 mg of molten bees wax that was clean or mixed with pesticides in treatment groups. The pesticides used were: C (Control-No Pesticide), A (Amitraz-~46,820 PPB), CC (Chlorothalonil + Chlorphyrifos - 53,700:890 PPB), FC (Fluvalinate + Coumophos - 20,400:93,200 PPB). 2. GRAFTED LARVAE TO RAISE QUEENS: One-day-old worker larvae were carefully transferred to the wax-coated queen cups. Developing queen cells were then placed into queenless cell builders with nurse workers that reared the future queens. 3. TRANSFERED NEW QUEENS TO NEW COLONIES: Emerged queens were individually transferred to mating nucleus colonies to allow them to mate naturally and lay fertilized eggs. 4. CAPTUREED QUEENS AND ISOLATE BRAIN: Mated queens were sacrificed and brains were dissected into Direct-zol(TM). 5. RNA EXTRACTION: RNA isolated using Direct-zol(TM) and glycogen as a coprecipitator. Quantified via HS RNA Qubit 2.0.

exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: thorax of young adult treated with Imidacloprid at a target dose of 1000 pg per bee for 24H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: fat-body of young adult treated with Fipronil at a target dose of 30 pg per bee for 24H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

study_title: Transcriptomic analysis of the honey bee (Apis mellifera) queen brain in response to pesticide exposure during development
design: 1. CONTAMINATED QUEEN CUPS WITH PESTICIDES: Plastic queen cups were coated with 200 mg of molten bees wax that was clean or mixed with pesticides in treatment groups. The pesticides used were: C (Control-No Pesticide), A (Amitraz-~46,820 PPB), CC (Chlorothalonil + Chlorphyrifos - 53,700:890 PPB), FC (Fluvalinate + Coumophos - 20,400:93,200 PPB). 2. GRAFTED LARVAE TO RAISE QUEENS: One-day-old worker larvae were carefully transferred to the wax-coated queen cups. Developing queen cells were then placed into queenless cell builders with nurse workers that reared the future queens. 3. TRANSFERED NEW QUEENS TO NEW COLONIES: Emerged queens were individually transferred to mating nucleus colonies to allow them to mate naturally and lay fertilized eggs. 4. CAPTUREED QUEENS AND ISOLATE BRAIN: Mated queens were sacrificed and brains were dissected into Direct-zol(TM). 5. RNA EXTRACTION: RNA isolated using Direct-zol(TM) and glycogen as a coprecipitator. Quantified via HS RNA Qubit 2.0.

exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: thorax of young adult treated with Fipronil at a target dose of 0.3 pg per bee for 48H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

study_title: Effect of the fungicide pyraclostrobin on the transcriptome of Africanized honey bees Apis mellifera during their foraging phase
exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: adult brain tissue
design: The total RNA was extracted from the brain pools of nurse bees A. mellifera using the TRIzol reagent protocol according to the manufacturers instructions (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). RNA concentrations were quantified in each sample using a QubitTM 2.0 fluorometer (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, USA). The levels of degradation of RNA were assessed by a 1% agarose gel. The cDNA libraries were constructed with 200 ng of total RNA using the SureSelect Strand Specific RNA Library Preparation Kit (Agilent Technologies, Santa Clara, USA), following the manufacturers instructions. The products from the library were then sequenced using an Illumina Nextseq platform (Illumina, San Diego, USA) in a single-bore run of 150 bp.

study_title: Transcriptomic analysis of the honey bee (Apis mellifera) queen brain in response to pesticide exposure during development
design: 1. CONTAMINATED QUEEN CUPS WITH PESTICIDES: Plastic queen cups were coated with 200 mg of molten bees wax that was clean or mixed with pesticides in treatment groups. The pesticides used were: C (Control-No Pesticide), A (Amitraz-~46,820 PPB), CC (Chlorothalonil + Chlorphyrifos - 53,700:890 PPB), FC (Fluvalinate + Coumophos - 20,400:93,200 PPB). 2. GRAFTED LARVAE TO RAISE QUEENS: One-day-old worker larvae were carefully transferred to the wax-coated queen cups. Developing queen cells were then placed into queenless cell builders with nurse workers that reared the future queens. 3. TRANSFERED NEW QUEENS TO NEW COLONIES: Emerged queens were individually transferred to mating nucleus colonies to allow them to mate naturally and lay fertilized eggs. 4. CAPTUREED QUEENS AND ISOLATE BRAIN: Mated queens were sacrificed and brains were dissected into Direct-zol(TM). 5. RNA EXTRACTION: RNA isolated using Direct-zol(TM) and glycogen as a coprecipitator. Quantified via HS RNA Qubit 2.0.

study_title: Transcriptomic analysis of the honey bee (Apis mellifera) queen brain in response to pesticide exposure during development
design: 1. CONTAMINATED QUEEN CUPS WITH PESTICIDES: Plastic queen cups were coated with 200 mg of molten bees wax that was clean or mixed with pesticides in treatment groups. The pesticides used were: C (Control-No Pesticide), A (Amitraz-~46,820 PPB), CC (Chlorothalonil + Chlorphyrifos - 53,700:890 PPB), FC (Fluvalinate + Coumophos - 20,400:93,200 PPB). 2. GRAFTED LARVAE TO RAISE QUEENS: One-day-old worker larvae were carefully transferred to the wax-coated queen cups. Developing queen cells were then placed into queenless cell builders with nurse workers that reared the future queens. 3. TRANSFERED NEW QUEENS TO NEW COLONIES: Emerged queens were individually transferred to mating nucleus colonies to allow them to mate naturally and lay fertilized eggs. 4. CAPTUREED QUEENS AND ISOLATE BRAIN: Mated queens were sacrificed and brains were dissected into Direct-zol(TM). 5. RNA EXTRACTION: RNA isolated using Direct-zol(TM) and glycogen as a coprecipitator. Quantified via HS RNA Qubit 2.0.

study_title: Transcriptomic analysis of the honey bee (Apis mellifera) queen brain in response to pesticide exposure during development
design: 1. CONTAMINATED QUEEN CUPS WITH PESTICIDES: Plastic queen cups were coated with 200 mg of molten bees wax that was clean or mixed with pesticides in treatment groups. The pesticides used were: C (Control-No Pesticide), A (Amitraz-~46,820 PPB), CC (Chlorothalonil + Chlorphyrifos - 53,700:890 PPB), FC (Fluvalinate + Coumophos - 20,400:93,200 PPB). 2. GRAFTED LARVAE TO RAISE QUEENS: One-day-old worker larvae were carefully transferred to the wax-coated queen cups. Developing queen cells were then placed into queenless cell builders with nurse workers that reared the future queens. 3. TRANSFERED NEW QUEENS TO NEW COLONIES: Emerged queens were individually transferred to mating nucleus colonies to allow them to mate naturally and lay fertilized eggs. 4. CAPTUREED QUEENS AND ISOLATE BRAIN: Mated queens were sacrificed and brains were dissected into Direct-zol(TM). 5. RNA EXTRACTION: RNA isolated using Direct-zol(TM) and glycogen as a coprecipitator. Quantified via HS RNA Qubit 2.0.

Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

Transcriptomic analysis of the honey bee (Apis mellifera) queen brain in response to pesticide exposure during development
design: 1. CONTAMINATED QUEEN CUPS WITH PESTICIDES: Plastic queen cups were coated with 200 mg of molten bees wax that was clean or mixed with pesticides in treatment groups. The pesticides used were: C (Control-No Pesticide), A (Amitraz-~46,820 PPB), CC (Chlorothalonil + Chlorphyrifos - 53,700:890 PPB), FC (Fluvalinate + Coumophos - 20,400:93,200 PPB). 2. GRAFTED LARVAE TO RAISE QUEENS: One-day-old worker larvae were carefully transferred to the wax-coated queen cups. Developing queen cells were then placed into queenless cell builders with nurse workers that reared the future queens. 3. TRANSFERED NEW QUEENS TO NEW COLONIES: Emerged queens were individually transferred to mating nucleus colonies to allow them to mate naturally and lay fertilized eggs. 4. CAPTUREED QUEENS AND ISOLATE BRAIN: Mated queens were sacrificed and brains were dissected into Direct-zol(TM). 5. RNA EXTRACTION: RNA isolated using Direct-zol(TM) and glycogen as a coprecipitator. Quantified via HS RNA Qubit 2.0.

study_title: Apis mellifera Transcriptome or Gene expression
exp_title: fat bodies from 3d bees fed control diet (honey and pollen)
design: Newly emerged bees (equal to or younger than 18 hours of age) were distributed among three replicate colonies. For each colony, bees were randomly distributed into each of two dietary treatments: a diet of honey and stored pollen (the control diet) or honey alone (pollen-restricted diet). Bees were restricted to each diet for a period of either 3 days or 8 days per colony for each diet. Fat bodies were isolated from five bees for each colony by diet by age combination and RNA was extracted using TriReagent according to the manufacturers protocols. Total RNA integrity was confirmed using Agilent’s 2100 Bioanalyzer before the samples were subjected to RNA-seq TruSeq library preparation.    Each RNA-Seq library (a total of 12 libraries were sequenced for the study: 2 ages x 2 diets x 3 replicate colonies) were prepared using Illumina’s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit according to the manufacturer’s protocols. Briefly, poly-A containing mRNA was purified using poly-T oligo-attached magnetic beads and was subsequently fragmented and primed for first-strand cDNA synthesis with random hexamer primers. After degrading the RNA template used for first-strand cDNA synthesis, synthesis of the second cDNA strand followed, yielding a double-stranded cDNA molecule. Adapters were ligated to both ends of the double-stranded cDNA molecules and adapter-ligated cDNAs were enriched using 15 cycles of PCR using adapter-specific primers. The libraries were validated according to the manufacturer’s protocol and the approximately 350 bp fragments were isolated from a 6% TBE PAGE gel and run through the Illumina sequencer for 2 x 100 cycles at a starting concentration of 12 pM per library.

study_title: Transcriptomic analysis for hygienic behavior in African-derived Apis mellifera honeybees
exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: hygienic behavior
design: Libraries were prepared with TruSeq RNA sample preparation kit (Illumina) at the Iowa State University DNA Facility. Total RNA was purified to obtain the mRNA, using polyT oligoattached magnetic beads, and then fragmented it. RNA fragments primed with random hexamers was reverse transcribed into first strand cDNA using reverse transcriptase (SuperScript II) and random primers. Then the cDNA was converted into double stranded DNA and subjected to library preparation. The 10-dsDNA samples were attached to adapters for hybridization onto a flow cell and amplified by PCR. The quality of the libraries was controlled by an Agilent Technologies 2100 Bioanalyzer with Agilent DNA1000 kit. Before sequencing, all samples were normalized and pooled together using the adapter indices provided in the kit.  Samples were sequenced in single-end read mode (1x), with 100 bp reads, on a HiSeq 3000 machine (Illumina).

study_title: Apis mellifera Transcriptome or Gene expression
exp_title: fat bodies from 8d bees fed pollen-restricted diet (honey only, no pollen) from colony K14
design: Newly emerged bees (equal to or younger than 18 hours of age) were distributed among three replicate colonies. For each colony, bees were randomly distributed into each of two dietary treatments: a diet of honey and stored pollen (the control diet) or honey alone (pollen-restricted diet). Bees were restricted to each diet for a period of either 3 days or 8 days per colony for each diet. Fat bodies were isolated from five bees for each colony by diet by age combination and RNA was extracted using TriReagent according to the manufacturers protocols. Total RNA integrity was confirmed using Agilent’s 2100 Bioanalyzer before the samples were subjected to RNA-seq TruSeq library preparation.    Each RNA-Seq library (a total of 12 libraries were sequenced for the study: 2 ages x 2 diets x 3 replicate colonies) were prepared using Illumina’s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit according to the manufacturer’s protocols. Briefly, poly-A containing mRNA was purified using poly-T oligo-attached magnetic beads and was subsequently fragmented and primed for first-strand cDNA synthesis with random hexamer primers. After degrading the RNA template used for first-strand cDNA synthesis, synthesis of the second cDNA strand followed, yielding a double-stranded cDNA molecule. Adapters were ligated to both ends of the double-stranded cDNA molecules and adapter-ligated cDNAs were enriched using 15 cycles of PCR using adapter-specific primers. The libraries were validated according to the manufacturer’s protocol and the approximately 350 bp fragments were isolated from a 6% TBE PAGE gel and run through the Illumina sequencer for 2 x 100 cycles at a starting concentration of 12 pM per library.

study_title: Transcriptomic analysis of the honey bee (Apis mellifera) queen brain in response to pesticide exposure during development
design: 1. CONTAMINATED QUEEN CUPS WITH PESTICIDES: Plastic queen cups were coated with 200 mg of molten bees wax that was clean or mixed with pesticides in treatment groups. The pesticides used were: C (Control-No Pesticide), A (Amitraz-~46,820 PPB), CC (Chlorothalonil + Chlorphyrifos - 53,700:890 PPB), FC (Fluvalinate + Coumophos - 20,400:93,200 PPB). 2. GRAFTED LARVAE TO RAISE QUEENS: One-day-old worker larvae were carefully transferred to the wax-coated queen cups. Developing queen cells were then placed into queenless cell builders with nurse workers that reared the future queens. 3. TRANSFERED NEW QUEENS TO NEW COLONIES: Emerged queens were individually transferred to mating nucleus colonies to allow them to mate naturally and lay fertilized eggs. 4. CAPTUREED QUEENS AND ISOLATE BRAIN: Mated queens were sacrificed and brains were dissected into Direct-zol(TM). 5. RNA EXTRACTION: RNA isolated using Direct-zol(TM) and glycogen as a coprecipitator. Quantified via HS RNA Qubit 2.0.

study_title: Apis mellifera Transcriptome or Gene expression
exp_title: fat bodies from 3d bees fed pollen-restricted diet (honey only, no pollen) from colony K3
design: Newly emerged bees (equal to or younger than 18 hours of age) were distributed among three replicate colonies. For each colony, bees were randomly distributed into each of two dietary treatments: a diet of honey and stored pollen (the control diet) or honey alone (pollen-restricted diet). Bees were restricted to each diet for a period of either 3 days or 8 days per colony for each diet. Fat bodies were isolated from five bees for each colony by diet by age combination and RNA was extracted using TriReagent according to the manufacturers protocols. Total RNA integrity was confirmed using Agilent’s 2100 Bioanalyzer before the samples were subjected to RNA-seq TruSeq library preparation.    Each RNA-Seq library (a total of 12 libraries were sequenced for the study: 2 ages x 2 diets x 3 replicate colonies) were prepared using Illumina’s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit according to the manufacturer’s protocols. Briefly, poly-A containing mRNA was purified using poly-T oligo-attached magnetic beads and was subsequently fragmented and primed for first-strand cDNA synthesis with random hexamer primers. After degrading the RNA template used for first-strand cDNA synthesis, synthesis of the second cDNA strand followed, yielding a double-stranded cDNA molecule. Adapters were ligated to both ends of the double-stranded cDNA molecules and adapter-ligated cDNAs were enriched using 15 cycles of PCR using adapter-specific primers. The libraries were validated according to the manufacturer’s protocol and the approximately 350 bp fragments were isolated from a 6% TBE PAGE gel and run through the Illumina sequencer for 2 x 100 cycles at a starting concentration of 12 pM per library.

study_title: Transcriptomic analysis of the honey bee (Apis mellifera) queen brain in response to pesticide exposure during development
design: 1. CONTAMINATED QUEEN CUPS WITH PESTICIDES: Plastic queen cups were coated with 200 mg of molten bees wax that was clean or mixed with pesticides in treatment groups. The pesticides used were: C (Control-No Pesticide), A (Amitraz-~46,820 PPB), CC (Chlorothalonil + Chlorphyrifos - 53,700:890 PPB), FC (Fluvalinate + Coumophos - 20,400:93,200 PPB). 2. GRAFTED LARVAE TO RAISE QUEENS: One-day-old worker larvae were carefully transferred to the wax-coated queen cups. Developing queen cells were then placed into queenless cell builders with nurse workers that reared the future queens. 3. TRANSFERED NEW QUEENS TO NEW COLONIES: Emerged queens were individually transferred to mating nucleus colonies to allow them to mate naturally and lay fertilized eggs. 4. CAPTUREED QUEENS AND ISOLATE BRAIN: Mated queens were sacrificed and brains were dissected into Direct-zol(TM). 5. RNA EXTRACTION: RNA isolated using Direct-zol(TM) and glycogen as a coprecipitator. Quantified via HS RNA Qubit 2.0.

exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: hygienic behavior
design: Libraries were prepared with TruSeq RNA sample preparation kit (Illumina) at the Iowa State University DNA Facility. Total RNA was purified to obtain the mRNA, using polyT oligoattached magnetic beads, and then fragmented it. RNA fragments primed with random hexamers was reverse transcribed into first strand cDNA using reverse transcriptase (SuperScript II) and random primers. Then the cDNA was converted into double stranded DNA and subjected to library preparation. The 10-dsDNA samples were attached to adapters for hybridization onto a flow cell and amplified by PCR. The quality of the libraries was controlled by an Agilent Technologies 2100 Bioanalyzer with Agilent DNA1000 kit. Before sequencing, all samples were normalized and pooled together using the adapter indices provided in the kit.  Samples were sequenced in single-end read mode (1x), with 100 bp reads, on a HiSeq 3000 machine (Illumina).

exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: nurse worker fat body
design: Total RNA was extracted using an RNeasy column (Qiagen). Total RNA integrity was confirmed with an Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer. RNA-seq libraries were prepared for the six samples using Illumina s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit as described in Corby-Harris et al. (2014). The resulting fragments of RNA were sequenced using an Illumina HiSeq 2500 and yielded 100 base pair paired-end sequences. The RNA was checked, the libraries were constructed, and the sequencing was performed at the University of Arizona UAGC (http://uagc.arl.arizona.edu/).

design: Total RNA was isolated from individual whole frozen larvae collected at 192hpf withTRIzol/chloroform extraction followed by precipitation with isopropanol and treated with 1ul (2U/ul) TURBOTM DNase (Invitrogen). Libraries were prepared from 1ug total RNA using Illumina Stranded mRNA Prep kits with unique 5 and 3 index adapter pairs (IDT for Illumina RNA UD Indexes, Set A) and PCR amplified for 10 cycles. Samples were sequenced on a NextSeq 2000 platform by the Penn State Genomics Core Facility.

exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: abdomen of young adult treated with Imidacloprid at a target dose of 1 pg per bee for 24H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

study_title: Apis mellifera Transcriptome or Gene expression
exp_title: fat bodies from 3d bees fed pollen-restricted diet (honey alone, no pollen) from colony K4
design: Newly emerged bees (equal to or younger than 18 hours of age) were distributed among three replicate colonies. For each colony, bees were randomly distributed into each of two dietary treatments: a diet of honey and stored pollen (the control diet) or honey alone (pollen-restricted diet). Bees were restricted to each diet for a period of either 3 days or 8 days per colony for each diet. Fat bodies were isolated from five bees for each colony by diet by age combination and RNA was extracted using TriReagent according to the manufacturers protocols. Total RNA integrity was confirmed using Agilent’s 2100 Bioanalyzer before the samples were subjected to RNA-seq TruSeq library preparation.    Each RNA-Seq library (a total of 12 libraries were sequenced for the study: 2 ages x 2 diets x 3 replicate colonies) were prepared using Illumina’s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit according to the manufacturer’s protocols. Briefly, poly-A containing mRNA was purified using poly-T oligo-attached magnetic beads and was subsequently fragmented and primed for first-strand cDNA synthesis with random hexamer primers. After degrading the RNA template used for first-strand cDNA synthesis, synthesis of the second cDNA strand followed, yielding a double-stranded cDNA molecule. Adapters were ligated to both ends of the double-stranded cDNA molecules and adapter-ligated cDNAs were enriched using 15 cycles of PCR using adapter-specific primers. The libraries were validated according to the manufacturer’s protocol and the approximately 350 bp fragments were isolated from a 6% TBE PAGE gel and run through the Illumina sequencer for 2 x 100 cycles at a starting concentration of 12 pM per library.

exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: hygienic behavior
design: Libraries were prepared with TruSeq RNA sample preparation kit (Illumina) at the Iowa State University DNA Facility. Total RNA was purified to obtain the mRNA, using polyT oligoattached magnetic beads, and then fragmented it. RNA fragments primed with random hexamers was reverse transcribed into first strand cDNA using reverse transcriptase (SuperScript II) and random primers. Then the cDNA was converted into double stranded DNA and subjected to library preparation. The 10-dsDNA samples were attached to adapters for hybridization onto a flow cell and amplified by PCR. The quality of the libraries was controlled by an Agilent Technologies 2100 Bioanalyzer with Agilent DNA1000 kit. Before sequencing, all samples were normalized and pooled together using the adapter indices provided in the kit.  Samples were sequenced in single-end read mode (1x), with 100 bp reads, on a HiSeq 3000 machine (Illumina).
lab: LASA/Centro de Pesquisa de Sanidade Animal/Institu

study_title: Apis mellifera Transcriptome or Gene expression
exp_title: fat bodies from 8d bees fed control diet (honey and pollen) from colony K3
design: Newly emerged bees (equal to or younger than 18 hours of age) were distributed among three replicate colonies. For each colony, bees were randomly distributed into each of two dietary treatments: a diet of honey and stored pollen (the control diet) or honey alone (pollen-restricted diet). Bees were restricted to each diet for a period of either 3 days or 8 days per colony for each diet. Fat bodies were isolated from five bees for each colony by diet by age combination and RNA was extracted using TriReagent according to the manufacturers protocols. Total RNA integrity was confirmed using Agilent’s 2100 Bioanalyzer before the samples were subjected to RNA-seq TruSeq library preparation.    Each RNA-Seq library (a total of 12 libraries were sequenced for the study: 2 ages x 2 diets x 3 replicate colonies) were prepared using Illumina’s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit according to the manufacturer’s protocols. Briefly, poly-A containing mRNA was purified using poly-T oligo-attached magnetic beads and was subsequently fragmented and primed for first-strand cDNA synthesis with random hexamer primers. After degrading the RNA template used for first-strand cDNA synthesis, synthesis of the second cDNA strand followed, yielding a double-stranded cDNA molecule. Adapters were ligated to both ends of the double-stranded cDNA molecules and adapter-ligated cDNAs were enriched using 15 cycles of PCR using adapter-specific primers. The libraries were validated according to the manufacturer’s protocol and the approximately 350 bp fragments were isolated from a 6% TBE PAGE gel and run through the Illumina sequencer for 2 x 100 cycles at a starting concentration of 12 pM per library.

study_title: New Approach Methodologies for Understanding Honey Bee Toxicity
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: thorax of young adult treated with Imidacloprid at a target dose of 1 pg per bee for 24H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

study_title: Apis mellifera Transcriptome or Gene expression
exp_title: fat bodies from 8d bees fed pollen-restricted diet (honey only, no pollen) from colony K4
design: Newly emerged bees (equal to or younger than 18 hours of age) were distributed among three replicate colonies. For each colony, bees were randomly distributed into each of two dietary treatments: a diet of honey and stored pollen (the control diet) or honey alone (pollen-restricted diet). Bees were restricted to each diet for a period of either 3 days or 8 days per colony for each diet. Fat bodies were isolated from five bees for each colony by diet by age combination and RNA was extracted using TriReagent according to the manufacturers protocols. Total RNA integrity was confirmed using Agilent’s 2100 Bioanalyzer before the samples were subjected to RNA-seq TruSeq library preparation.    Each RNA-Seq library (a total of 12 libraries were sequenced for the study: 2 ages x 2 diets x 3 replicate colonies) were prepared using Illumina’s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit according to the manufacturer’s protocols. Briefly, poly-A containing mRNA was purified using poly-T oligo-attached magnetic beads and was subsequently fragmented and primed for first-strand cDNA synthesis with random hexamer primers. After degrading the RNA template used for first-strand cDNA synthesis, synthesis of the second cDNA strand followed, yielding a double-stranded cDNA molecule. Adapters were ligated to both ends of the double-stranded cDNA molecules and adapter-ligated cDNAs were enriched using 15 cycles of PCR using adapter-specific primers. The libraries were validated according to the manufacturer’s protocol and the approximately 350 bp fragments were isolated from a 6% TBE PAGE gel and run through the Illumina sequencer for 2 x 100 cycles at a starting concentration of 12 pM per library.

exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: hygienic behavior
design: Libraries were prepared with TruSeq RNA sample preparation kit (Illumina) at the Iowa State University DNA Facility. Total RNA was purified to obtain the mRNA, using polyT oligoattached magnetic beads, and then fragmented it. RNA fragments primed with random hexamers was reverse transcribed into first strand cDNA using reverse transcriptase (SuperScript II) and random primers. Then the cDNA was converted into double stranded DNA and subjected to library preparation. The 10-dsDNA samples were attached to adapters for hybridization onto a flow cell and amplified by PCR. The quality of the libraries was controlled by an Agilent Technologies 2100 Bioanalyzer with Agilent DNA1000 kit. Before sequencing, all samples were normalized and pooled together using the adapter indices provided in the kit.  Samples were sequenced in single-end read mode (1x), with 100 bp reads, on a HiSeq 3000 machine (Illumina).

treatment: fed a control diet containing pollen and honey
study_title: Apis mellifera Transcriptome or Gene expression
exp_title: fat bodies from 8d bees fed control diet (honey and pollen) from colony K14
design: Newly emerged bees (equal to or younger than 18 hours of age) were distributed among three replicate colonies. For each colony, bees were randomly distributed into each of two dietary treatments: a diet of honey and stored pollen (the control diet) or honey alone (pollen-restricted diet). Bees were restricted to each diet for a period of either 3 days or 8 days per colony for each diet. Fat bodies were isolated from five bees for each colony by diet by age combination and RNA was extracted using TriReagent according to the manufacturers protocols. Total RNA integrity was confirmed using Agilent’s 2100 Bioanalyzer before the samples were subjected to RNA-seq TruSeq library preparation.    Each RNA-Seq library (a total of 12 libraries were sequenced for the study: 2 ages x 2 diets x 3 replicate colonies) were prepared using Illumina’s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit according to the manufacturer’s protocols. Briefly, poly-A containing mRNA was purified using poly-T oligo-attached magnetic beads and was subsequently fragmented and primed for first-strand cDNA synthesis with random hexamer primers. After degrading the RNA template used for first-strand cDNA synthesis, synthesis of the second cDNA strand followed, yielding a double-stranded cDNA molecule. Adapters were ligated to both ends of the double-stranded cDNA molecules and adapter-ligated cDNAs were enriched using 15 cycles of PCR using adapter-specific primers. The libraries were validated according to the manufacturer’s protocol and the approximately 350 bp fragments were isolated from a 6% TBE PAGE gel and run through the Illumina sequencer for 2 x 100 cycles at a starting concentration of 12 pM per library.

study_title: Transcriptomic analysis for hygienic behavior in African-derived Apis mellifera honeybees
exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: hygienic behavior
design: Libraries were prepared with TruSeq RNA sample preparation kit (Illumina) at the Iowa State University DNA Facility. Total RNA was purified to obtain the mRNA, using polyT oligoattached magnetic beads, and then fragmented it. RNA fragments primed with random hexamers was reverse transcribed into first strand cDNA using reverse transcriptase (SuperScript II) and random primers. Then the cDNA was converted into double stranded DNA and subjected to library preparation. The 10-dsDNA samples were attached to adapters for hybridization onto a flow cell and amplified by PCR. The quality of the libraries was controlled by an Agilent Technologies 2100 Bioanalyzer with Agilent DNA1000 kit. Before sequencing, all samples were normalized and pooled together using the adapter indices provided in the kit.  Samples were sequenced in single-end read mode (1x), with 100 bp reads, on a HiSeq 3000 machine (Illumina).

study_title: Apis mellifera Transcriptome or Gene expression
exp_title: fat bodies from 3d bees fed control diet (honey and pollen) colony K14
design: Newly emerged bees (equal to or younger than 18 hours of age) were distributed among three replicate colonies. For each colony, bees were randomly distributed into each of two dietary treatments: a diet of honey and stored pollen (the control diet) or honey alone (pollen-restricted diet). Bees were restricted to each diet for a period of either 3 days or 8 days per colony for each diet. Fat bodies were isolated from five bees for each colony by diet by age combination and RNA was extracted using TriReagent according to the manufacturers protocols. Total RNA integrity was confirmed using Agilent’s 2100 Bioanalyzer before the samples were subjected to RNA-seq TruSeq library preparation.    Each RNA-Seq library (a total of 12 libraries were sequenced for the study: 2 ages x 2 diets x 3 replicate colonies) were prepared using Illumina’s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit according to the manufacturer’s protocols. Briefly, poly-A containing mRNA was purified using poly-T oligo-attached magnetic beads and was subsequently fragmented and primed for first-strand cDNA synthesis with random hexamer primers. After degrading the RNA template used for first-strand cDNA synthesis, synthesis of the second cDNA strand followed, yielding a double-stranded cDNA molecule. Adapters were ligated to both ends of the double-stranded cDNA molecules and adapter-ligated cDNAs were enriched using 15 cycles of PCR using adapter-specific primers. The libraries were validated according to the manufacturer’s protocol and the approximately 350 bp fragments were isolated from a 6% TBE PAGE gel and run through the Illumina sequencer for 2 x 100 cycles at a starting concentration of 12 pM per library.

design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

study_title: RNA-seq of imaginal discs during metamorphosis to wings and thoracic dorsum in the honeybee castes
exp_title: RNA-seq of honey bee workers: wings and thoracic dorsum
design: RNA extractions were made using TRIzol reagent (Invitrogen) following manufacturer instructions. Purity and concentration of RNA extracted from each sample was determined through optical absorbance at 260/280 nm using NanoDrop ND-1000 (NanoDrop Technologies). The RNA samples (2 g/per sample) were used for libraries preparation using TruSeq RNA sample preparation (Illumina) and a system of adaptors (barcodes). RNA samples were sequenced in an Illumina HiSeq 2500 equipment.

exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: nurse worker fat body
design: Total RNA was extracted using an RNeasy column (Qiagen). Total RNA integrity was confirmed with an Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer. RNA-seq libraries were prepared for the six samples using Illumina s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit as described in Corby-Harris et al. (2014). The resulting fragments of RNA were sequenced using an Illumina HiSeq 2500 and yielded 100 base pair paired-end sequences. The RNA was checked, the libraries were constructed, and the sequencing was performed at the University of Arizona UAGC (http://uagc.arl.arizona.edu/).

Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

study_title: New Approach Methodologies for Understanding Honey Bee Toxicity
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: abdomen of young adult treated with Imidacloprid at a target dose of 10000 pg per bee for 24H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

study_title: Apis mellifera Transcriptome or Gene expression
exp_title: fat bodies from 3d bees fed control diet (honey and pollen)
design: Newly emerged bees (equal to or younger than 18 hours of age) were distributed among three replicate colonies. For each colony, bees were randomly distributed into each of two dietary treatments: a diet of honey and stored pollen (the control diet) or honey alone (pollen-restricted diet). Bees were restricted to each diet for a period of either 3 days or 8 days per colony for each diet. Fat bodies were isolated from five bees for each colony by diet by age combination and RNA was extracted using TriReagent according to the manufacturers protocols. Total RNA integrity was confirmed using Agilent’s 2100 Bioanalyzer before the samples were subjected to RNA-seq TruSeq library preparation.Each RNA-Seq library (a total of 12 libraries were sequenced for the study: 2 ages x 2 diets x 3 replicate colonies) were prepared using Illumina’s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit according to the manufacturer’s protocols. Briefly, poly-A containing mRNA was purified using poly-T oligo-attached magnetic beads and was subsequently fragmented and primed for first-strand cDNA synthesis with random hexamer primers. After degrading the RNA template used for first-strand cDNA synthesis, synthesis of the second cDNA strand followed, yielding a double-stranded cDNA molecule. Adapters were ligated to both ends of the double-stranded cDNA molecules and adapter-ligated cDNAs were enriched using 15 cycles of PCR using adapter-specific primers. The libraries were validated according to the manufacturer’s protocol and the approximately 350 bp fragments were isolated from a 6% TBE PAGE gel and run through the Illumina sequencer for 2 x 100 cycles at a starting concentration of 12 pM per library.

Impacts of phytochemical diversity of honey on lifespan, gene expression, and pesticide tolerance in the honey bee
exp_title: 14-day-old Apis mellifera fed on sugar rep 3
design: Day-old adult bees were assigned to five different honey diet treatments: tupelo, locust, and buckwheat honeys individually, a free-choice three-honey treatment (TLB-Choice) and sugar control. For this experiment, all diets contained casein at a ratio of 1:12 protein to carbohydrate. After 14 days, surviving bees were chilled on ice and midguts were individually removed and flash-frozen in liquid nitrogen. The 14-day termination date was selected because it represents an approximate midpoint in a typical adult lifespan (55) and typically involves performing in-hive tasks, including comb building, food processing, and food storage (56, 57)

Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

design: Total RNA was extracted using an RNeasy column (Qiagen). Total RNA integrity was confirmed with an Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer. The resulting fragments of RNA were sequenced using an Illumina HiSeq 2500 and yielded 100 base pair paired-end sequences. The RNA was checked, the libraries were constructed, and the sequencing was performed at the University of Arizona UAGC (http://uagc.arl.arizona.edu/).

study_title: Parent-of-origin effects on transcription and chromatin regulation in reproductive caste determination
design: Total RNA was isolated from individual whole frozen larvae collected at 192hpf withTRIzol/chloroform extraction followed by precipitation with isopropanol and treated with 1ul (2U/ul) TURBOTM DNase (Invitrogen). Libraries were prepared from 1ug total RNA using Illumina Stranded mRNA Prep kits with unique 5 and 3 index adapter pairs (IDT for Illumina RNA UD Indexes, Set A) and PCR amplified for 10 cycles. Libraries were sequenced on a NextSeq 2000 platform through the Penn State Genomics Research Incubator.
lab: Huck Institutes of the Life Sciences

exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: abdomen of young adult treated with Fipronil at a target dose of 300 pg per bee for 48H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

study_title: Transcriptional response of European foulbrood diseased Apis mellifera larvae
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera larva: Melissococcus plutonius infection EFB
design: Samples were collected on day 3 post infection (3 dpi) and day 6 post infection (6 dpi), respectively  Total RNA and DNA were sequentially isolated. Sampled individuals were homogenized in 300 l (3 dpi) and 600 l (6 dpi) extraction buffer, respectively. RNA was isolated using a NucleoSpin RNA purification kit (Macherey-Nagel, Dren, Germany) according to manufacturers protocol. Remaining genomic DNA was removed by digestion with TURBO DNase (Invitrogen, ThermoFischer Scientific, Paisley, United Kingdom). The Ribo-Zero magnetic kit (Epicentre Biotechnologies, Madison, WI, USA) was used to reduce the amount of bacterial-derived rRNA sequences. The NEBNext Poly(A) mRNA Magnetic Isolation Module (New England BioLabs, Frankfurt am Main, Germany) was used to remove eukaryotic-derived rRNA sequences. cDNA libraries were constructed with a NEBNext Ultra II RNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England BioLabs, Frankfurt am Main, Germany).  To assess quality and size of the libraries samples were run on an Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100 using an Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit as recommended by the manufacturer (Agilent Technologies, Waldbronn, Germany). Concentration of the libraries were determined using the Qubit dsDNA HS Assay Kit as recommended by the manufacturer (Life Technologies GmbH, Darmstadt, Germany).

study_title: Impacts of phytochemical diversity of honey on lifespan, gene expression, and pesticide tolerance in the honey bee
exp_title: 14-day-old Apis mellifera fed on locust  honey rep 1
design: Day-old adult bees were assigned to five different honey diet treatments: tupelo, locust, and buckwheat honeys individually, a free-choice three-honey treatment (TLB-Choice) and sugar control. For this experiment, all diets contained casein at a ratio of 1:12 protein to carbohydrate. After 14 days, surviving bees were chilled on ice and midguts were individually removed and flash-frozen in liquid nitrogen. The 14-day termination date was selected because it represents an approximate midpoint in a typical adult lifespan (55) and typically involves performing in-hive tasks, including comb building, food processing, and food storage (56, 57)

study_title: New Approach Methodologies for Understanding Honey Bee Toxicity
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: brain of young adult treated with Fipronil at a target dose of 0.3 pg per bee for 24H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

study_title: Impacts of phytochemical diversity of honey on lifespan, gene expression, and pesticide tolerance in the honey bee
exp_title: 14-day-old Apis mellifera fed on locust  honey rep 2
design: Day-old adult bees were assigned to five different honey diet treatments: tupelo, locust, and buckwheat honeys individually, a free-choice three-honey treatment (TLB-Choice) and sugar control. For this experiment, all diets contained casein at a ratio of 1:12 protein to carbohydrate. After 14 days, surviving bees were chilled on ice and midguts were individually removed and flash-frozen in liquid nitrogen. The 14-day termination date was selected because it represents an approximate midpoint in a typical adult lifespan (55) and typically involves performing in-hive tasks, including comb building, food processing, and food storage (56, 57)

study_title: HGs and diet transcriptome
design: Total RNA was extracted from each sample using TriReagent (Invitrogen) according to the manufacturers specifications. RNA integrity was confirmed using Agilents 2100 Bioanalyzer. 4 lg of total RNA from each the six RNA sequencing (RNA-Seq) libraries were prepared using Illuminas TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit. The sequencing libraries were validated and run through a 6% TBE PAGE gel. 50 bp fragments were isolated and run through the Illumina HiSeq 2000/2500 sequencer for 2  150 cycles at a starting concentration of 12 pM per library. All six samples were run on one lane.

study_title: Effects of cadmium on honeybees
design: The abdomens of 6~8 bees for each replicate were pooled as a sample for the total RNA extraction using Trizol reagent (Invitrogen, Carlsbad, USA). After quantification and quality control, a total of 3 g of total RNA was prepared to purify the Poly (A) mRNA using poly-T oligo-attached magnetic beads (Invitrogen). The mRNA was broken down into small pieces by divalent cations under elevated temperature, which were used to generate the sequencing library using a mRNA-Seq sample preparation kit (Illumina, San Diego, USA) following the manufacturers instruction, and the average insert size of the paired-end libraries ranged from 250 bp to 350 bp. The library was sequenced on Illumina Novaseq 6000 platform (Illumina), and 150 bp paired-end reads were generated.

study_title: Transcriptional response of European foulbrood diseased Apis mellifera larvae
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera larva: Melissococcus plutonius infection EFB
design: Samples were collected on day 3 post infection (3 dpi) and day 6 post infection (6 dpi), respectively  Total RNA and DNA were sequentially isolated. Sampled individuals were homogenized in 300 l (3 dpi) and 600 l (6 dpi) extraction buffer, respectively. RNA was isolated using a NucleoSpin RNA purification kit (Macherey-Nagel, Dren, Germany) according to manufacturers protocol. Remaining genomic DNA was removed by digestion with TURBO DNase (Invitrogen, ThermoFischer Scientific, Paisley, United Kingdom). The Ribo-Zero magnetic kit (Epicentre Biotechnologies, Madison, WI, USA) was used to reduce the amount of bacterial-derived rRNA sequences. The NEBNext Poly(A) mRNA Magnetic Isolation Module (New England BioLabs, Frankfurt am Main, Germany) was used to remove eukaryotic-derived rRNA sequences. cDNA libraries were constructed with a NEBNext Ultra II RNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England BioLabs, Frankfurt am Main, Germany).  To assess quality and size of the libraries samples were run on an Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100 using an Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit as recommended by the manufacturer (Agilent Technologies, Waldbronn, Germany). Concentration of the libraries were determined using the Qubit dsDNA HS Assay Kit as recommended by the manufacturer (Life Technologies GmbH, Darmstadt, Germany).

exp_title: Apis mellifera larvae inoculated with P. apium C6 rep 2
design: Total RNA was extracted using TriReagent (Invitrogen). Total RNA integrity was confirmed with an Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer. A small aliquot (5  l) of the RNA was retained for rtPCR (see below). With the remaining RNA, RNA-seq libraries were prepared for the six samples using Illumina s TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit as described in Corby-Harris et al. (2014). The resulting fragments of RNA were sequenced using an Illumina HiSeq 4000 and yielded 100 base pair paired-end sequences. The RNA was checked, the libraries were constructed, and the sequencing was performed at BGI Americas (https://www.bgi.com/us/).

exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: brain of young adult treated with Fipronil at a target dose of 300 pg per bee for 24H
design: Prior to sequencing library preparation, RNA samples  undergo fragment analysis using an Advanced Analytical Technologies Fragment Analyzer and PROSize 3.0 software (v. 4.0.2.7) (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA) for quality and quantity measurements.  Each gene expression sample is spiked with the Ambion ERCC Mix 1 (ThermoFisher Scientific, Inc.) at a ratio of 2 L of a 1:1000 dilution to 1 g total RNA, according to manufacturer instructions.   Sequencing libraries from total RNA are prepared using the Illumina Stranded mRNA Kit for mRNA Expression (cat. No 20040534 with IDT for Illumina UDI Indexes set A and B, Cat. No 20040553 and 20040554 respectively).  Briefly, mRNAs are isolated via attachment to oligo(dT) beads, chemically fragmented to a mean size of 150 nt, and reverse transcribed into cDNA via random hexamer priming.  Resulting double-stranded cDNA fragments are end-repaired to create blunt-end fragments, 3 adenine-tailed, ligated with indexed adapters, and PCR-amplified using Illumina primers.  Purified PCR-amplified libraries are checked for quality and quantity on an Agilent TapeStation 4200 using D1000 Screentape before normalization and sample pooling.  Pool is sequenced with an appropriate sized flowcell to target 20-25M reads per sample on either a NextSeq or a Novaseq depending on total sample number and total number of reads needed

design: Day-old adult bees were assigned to five different honey diet treatments: tupelo, locust, and buckwheat honeys individually, a free-choice three-honey treatment (TLB-Choice) and sugar control. For this experiment, all diets contained casein at a ratio of 1:12 protein to carbohydrate. After 14 days, surviving bees were chilled on ice and midguts were individually removed and flash-frozen in liquid nitrogen. The 14-day termination date was selected because it represents an approximate midpoint in a typical adult lifespan (55) and typically involves performing in-hive tasks, including comb building, food processing, and food storage (56, 57)

study_title: Transcriptomic analysis for hygienic behavior in African-derived Apis mellifera honeybees
exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: hygienic behavior
design: Libraries were prepared with TruSeq RNA sample preparation kit (Illumina) at the Iowa State University DNA Facility. Total RNA was purified to obtain the mRNA, using polyT oligoattached magnetic beads, and then fragmented it. RNA fragments primed with random hexamers was reverse transcribed into first strand cDNA using reverse transcriptase (SuperScript II) and random primers. Then the cDNA was converted into double stranded DNA and subjected to library preparation. The 10-dsDNA samples were attached to adapters for hybridization onto a flow cell and amplified by PCR. The quality of the libraries was controlled by an Agilent Technologies 2100 Bioanalyzer with Agilent DNA1000 kit. Before sequencing, all samples were normalized and pooled together using the adapter indices provided in the kit.  Samples were sequenced in single-end read mode (1x), with 100 bp reads, on a HiSeq 3000 machine (Illumina).

RNA integrity was confirmed using Agilents 2100 Bioanalyzer. 4 lg of total RNA from each the six RNA sequencing (RNA-Seq) libraries were prepared using Illuminas TruSeq RNA Sample Preparation Kit. All six samples were run on one lane.

exp_title: RNA-seq of Apis mellifera: hygienic behavior
design: Libraries were prepared with TruSeq RNA sample preparation kit (Illumina) at the Iowa State University DNA Facility. Total RNA was purified to obtain the mRNA, using polyT oligoattached magnetic beads, and then fragmented it. RNA fragments primed with random hexamers was reverse transcribed into first strand cDNA using reverse transcriptase (SuperScript II) and random primers. Then the cDNA was converted into double stranded DNA and subjected to library preparation. The 10-dsDNA samples were attached to adapters for hybridization onto a flow cell and amplified by PCR. The quality of the libraries was controlled by an Agilent Technologies 2100 Bioanalyzer with Agilent DNA1000 kit. Before sequencing, all samples were normalized and pooled together using the adapter indices provided in the kit.  Samples were sequenced in single-end read mode (1x), with 100 bp reads, on a HiSeq 3000 machine (Illumina).
lab: LASA/Centro de Pesquisa de Sanidade Animal/Institu

study_title: Impacts of phytochemical diversity of honey on lifespan, gene expression, and pesticide tolerance in the honey bee
exp_title: 14-day-old Apis mellifera fed on buckwheat honey rep 1
design: Day-old adult bees were assigned to five different honey diet treatments: tupelo, locust, and buckwheat honeys individually, a free-choice three-honey treatment (TLB-Choice) and sugar control. For this experiment, all diets contained casein at a ratio of 1:12 protein to carbohydrate. After 14 days, surviving bees were chilled on ice and midguts were individually removed and flash-frozen in liquid nitrogen. The 14-day termination date was selected because it represents an approximate midpoint in a typical adult lifespan (55) and typically involves performing in-hive tasks, including comb building, food processing, and food storage (56, 57)

study_title: Transcriptional response of European foulbrood diseased Apis mellifera larvae
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera larva: Melissococcus plutonius infection EFB
design: Samples were collected on day 3 post infection (3 dpi) and day 6 post infection (6 dpi), respectively  Total RNA and DNA were sequentially isolated. Sampled individuals were homogenized in 300 l (3 dpi) and 600 l (6 dpi) extraction buffer, respectively. RNA was isolated using a NucleoSpin RNA purification kit (Macherey-Nagel, Dren, Germany) according to manufacturers protocol. Remaining genomic DNA was removed by digestion with TURBO DNase (Invitrogen, ThermoFischer Scientific, Paisley, United Kingdom). The Ribo-Zero magnetic kit (Epicentre Biotechnologies, Madison, WI, USA) was used to reduce the amount of bacterial-derived rRNA sequences. The NEBNext Poly(A) mRNA Magnetic Isolation Module (New England BioLabs, Frankfurt am Main, Germany) was used to remove eukaryotic-derived rRNA sequences. cDNA libraries were constructed with a NEBNext Ultra II RNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England BioLabs, Frankfurt am Main, Germany).  To assess quality and size of the libraries samples were run on an Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100 using an Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit as recommended by the manufacturer (Agilent Technologies, Waldbronn, Germany). Concentration of the libraries were determined using the Qubit dsDNA HS Assay Kit as recommended by the manufacturer (Life Technologies GmbH, Darmstadt, Germany).
